trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Welcome to a new semester!
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 01/20/26

urls.page_summary: Disagreement is at the heart of the university and at the heart of democracies. In universities, scholars in every discipline engage in disagreement every day, from the nature of the outer reaches of the galaxy, to the value and meaning of differe...
urls.page_title: Nick Salvatore, ‘one of our foremost historians,’ dies at 82 | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Nick Salvatore, a professor emeritus in the ILR School, an award-winning historian and teacher and lifelong champion for working people, died on Nov. 29 in Ithaca. He was 82.
urls.page_title: The people behind the telescope: How specialized expertise is supporting science at the edge of the universe
urls.page_summary: Behind a world-leading telescope bound for Chile is a team of engineers, machinists, electronics specialists and riggers at Cornell. Meet the specialized staff whose expertise is helping push cosmology to new frontiers.
urls.page_title: Mészáros elected to American Mathematical Society
urls.page_summary: Mészáros’ research focuses on algebraic and geometric combinatorics.
urls.page_title: The mystery of the Seneca Drums persists | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Researchers are plumbing the depths of the largest and deepest of New York’s Finger Lakes to explain the source of its famous booming sounds.
urls.page_title: CCHK Salon: Their Swan Songs: Schubert and Stucky (CU Music) - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Pianist Xak Bjerken is joined by baritone Brian V. Sengdala for a concert of Schubert’s epic Schwanengesang as well as a performance of an unpublished song by Steven Stucky, his final work for voice entitled Out of the Cradle, Endlessly Rocking. T...
Awards and honors received by faculty, postdocs and graduate students in the College of Arts & Sciences.

The College of Arts & Sciences’ Arts Unplugged series brings research and creative works into the public sphere for discussion and inspiration. These engaging events invite a broad audience to explore the work of scholars, faculty and creative lum...
urls.page_title: Women in exile shaped South Africa’s anti-apartheid movement
urls.page_summary: Women played a major role in debates surrounding the fight against apartheid in South Africa, Rachel Sandwell writes in a new book, “National Liberation and the Political Life of Exile: Sex, Gender, and Nation in the Struggle against Apartheid.”
